channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russian prosecutors seek 7-year sentence for US man accused of fighting for Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian prosecutors asked for a seven-year sentence in the trial of a U.S. citizen accused of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine against Russia, Russian news agencies reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris is heading to North Carolina to survey Helene's aftermath one day after Trump visited
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris is heading to North Carolina on Saturday as the state recovers from Hurricane Helene, arriving there one day after a visit by Republican Donald Trump, who is spreading false claims about the federal response to the disaster.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Militants kill six Pakistani soldiers during a shootout in the country's northwest
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Militants killed six Pakistani soldiers during a shootout in the northwest, the army said Saturday. It’s the latest unrest in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, which borders Afghanistan and is a base for armed groups including the Pakistani Taliban.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rachel Morin's mom comes face-to-face with illegal immigrant murder suspect for first time in court
item.description: Rachel Morin's family came face-to-face for the first time with her suspected killer, an illegal immigrant who wore an "emotionless expression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio woman's viral videos about rug buried in her yard led to search for potential body
item.description: An Ohio woman shared videos on her TikTok, concerned that a body may have been buried in her yard, leading to a police search of her property.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man set to die this month is at the center of another push for clemency in the U.S., this time backed by several GOP lawmakers and bestselling author John Grisham, who say a father’s 2002 conviction for killing his infant daughter deserves a second look.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Francisco's first Black female mayor is in a pricey battle for a second term
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — When London Breed was elected as San Francisco's first Black woman mayor, it was a pinch-me moment for a poor girl from public housing whose ascension showed that no dream was impossible in the progressive, compassionate and equitable city.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Well-known Asheville music tradition returns in a sign of hopefulness after Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — A well-known Asheville musical tradition returned Friday night, in a sign of hopefulness a week after Helene battered the mountain city. The Asheville Drum Circle had its first regular Friday night session since the powerful storm blew in.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho state senator tells Native American candidate 'go back where you came from' in forum
item.description: KENDRICK, Idaho (AP) — Tensions rose during a bipartisan forum this week after an audience question about discrimination reportedly led an Idaho state senator to angrily tell a Native American candidate to “go back where you came from.” Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles mayor picks ex-sheriff to be city's police chief
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former LA County Sheriff Jim McDonnell has been selected to lead the Los Angeles Police Department, taking charge of the force of nearly 9,000 officers as discontent grows among the city's residents over public safety even as violent crime numbers drop, the mayor announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New judge could give Diddy another attempt to win bail
item.description: While Diddy's legal team hasn’t commented, the assignment of a new judge potentially opens the door for a third bail hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man charged with helping Idaho inmate escape during a hospital ambush sentenced to life in prison
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho man who prosecutors said ambushed and shot correctional officers at a Boise hospital in a plot to help a fellow white supremacist gang member escape from prison was sentenced to life behind bars on Friday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some perplexed at jury's mixed verdict in trial for 3 former officers in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Moments after a federal judge read the partial convictions in the federal trial of three former Memphis officers in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, an activist outside the courtroom could not contain his shock. “What the (expletive)?” Hunter Demster exclaimed to a reporter.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Satellite shows before-and-after flooding in North Carolina after Helene
item.description: Planet Labs captured and compared satellite images that show the storm's devastating impact on one of the hardest hit cities.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, WGN 9

item.title: Ohio woman's viral TikTok videos lead prompt search for body in her yard
item.description: Police in Columbus, Ohio have concluded an investigation after a woman posted on TikTok that she might have discovered a body buried in her yard.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Search dogs trained in Southern California deployed for Hurricane Helene rescue efforts
item.description: Dogs that have been trained in Ventura County to respond to international disasters have been deployed to the Southern U.S. to aid in Hurricane Helene recovery efforts. Eleven canine disaster search teams from all over the country have traveled to the most heavily affected areas to lend a paw.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, KRON4

item.title: Bizarre Minnesota laws, including penalties for driving a filthy car, that will shock you
item.description: Minnesota has quite a few strange laws on the books, such as one including mosquitoes and another preventing drivers to travel with dirty tires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump returning to site of assassination attempt for another rally
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, Fox 5

item.title: Biden: 'I don't know' if Netanyahu trying to influence election
item.description: President Biden on Friday said he wasn't aware of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was trying to influence the U.S. election, while noting no administration has been a stronger ally to Israel than his own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some Republicans embrace ‘pro-choice’ label, infuriating Democrats
item.description: A few Republicans running for competitive or Democratic-leaning congressional seats are adopting and reviving a label that was nearly extinct in their party: pro-choice.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Musk to attend Trump rally at site of attempted assassination
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he plans to attend former President Donald Trump’s rally in battleground Pennsylvania this weekend when Trump returns to the site where he survived the first assassination attempt on his life.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Helene’s powerful storm surge killed 12 near Tampa. They didn't have to die
item.description: INDIAN ROCKS BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Aiden Bowles was stubborn, so even as Florida officials told residents of the barrier island north of St. Petersburg that Hurricane Helene's storm surge could be deadly, the retired restaurant owner stayed put.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER and TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Need a passport? You can now get one faster
item.description: U.S. passport processing time has been reduced by two weeks at most, the State Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, ABC 4

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a makeup artist.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, NewsNation

item.title: Mayorkas warns FEMA doesn't have enough funding to last through hurricane season
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas says the agency tasked with helping communities recover from Hurricane Helene and other natural disasters does not have enough money to make it through the hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and COLLEEN LONG, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Immigrant murder convictions 'tens of thousands' higher than ICE's bombshell figures: data expert
item.description: The total number of noncitizens in the U.S. who have murder convictions is likely "tens of thousands" more than the 13,400 listed on Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s national docket.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Man has intense stare down with moose before it charges at him
item.description: A man who came face-to-face with a bull moose in the woodlands of Maine narrowly avoided a major mishap after the animal charged after him.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Milwaukee man accused of stealing from same Walgreens 3 separate times
item.description: John Dzwonkowski faces charges of stealing toilet paper, soaps, lotion, razors and household cleaning items.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
